Title: HWD General Info Session
Location: - Virtual Event
Description: Join us for a snapshot of the work Harriet’s Wildest Dreams is engaged in. Our liberation is tied to one another, and we need each other to do this work to protect our people — Black people, formerly incarcerated folks, queer and trans communities, and all those at risk of state-sanctioned violence. Being in the nation's capital, we are at the center of the empire and also a target for far-right extremists. DC is NO ONE’S playground, and we must remain vigilant in our fight for statehood, our fight against mass incarceration, and the fight for gender liberation and bodily autonomy for all. If you and your friends are interested in building a better world, free from patriarchal violence, a world where queerness is in, then you've come to the right place! Come sit with us as we re-imagine what this world can be and reclaim safety for all of DC. 👉🏽 This month's info session will feature a political education session on the history of surveillance and how it shows up in our city. How does policing build upon the surveillance state? What has surveillance looked like throughout our city? Find out during this month's general info session! Who Are We:

Title: ReproRave - Care Kit Packing Event
Location: - TRANSMISSION DC
Description: Party for a cause at ReproRave with MDC DSA’s Bodily Autonomy Working Group and EC4DC! Start your night by packing sexual and reproductive health care-kits for EC4DC before heading to the dance floor to party the night away with DJ Joyty and supporting DJs [TICKETS FOR RAVE SOLD SEPERATELY]. Please consider donating to our fundraiser here! Join us at 7:30PM as we begin folding zines for our care kit packing event. At 8:30PM, we will begin packing our care kits, which include a zine, a dose of emergency contraception, condoms, a pregnancy test, and more. These kits will be given out for free at Transmission DC and all around DC. For a full list of distribution sites, please visit EC4DC's website. 100% of your ticket cost (minus Action Network fees) will go towards funding these care kits. If you wish to donate more, please consider donating to our fundraiser here. EC4DC is a mutual aid project providing free emergency contraception to DC residents. Find more information on the EC 4 DC linktree. The Bodily Autonomy Working Group (BAWG) is a priority campaign of the Metro DC chapter of the Democratic Socialists of America committed to pursuing reproductive justice and trans and queer liberation. Please fill out our **interest form** if you're interested in joining our campaign.

Title: Deep Roots, Grow Power: ROC-DC's 2026 Relaunch & Fundraiser
Location: - The Festival Center
Description: Worker-members of ROC-DC, a collective that organizes workers in bars and restaurants across DC and played a pivotal role in the defense of Initiative 82, voted last year to change their name to the Restaurant Organizing Collective. This new name, now in effect, is an affirmation of what has always been true: ROC’s commitment to organizing, building, and sharing the power of DC’s restaurant workers. To commemorate the name change and raise funds for their efforts, ROC will be holding a fundraiser on Wednesday, May 20 at 5pm at the Adams Morgan Festival Center, replete with food, music, and dancing. Title: Black Girl Booked with Author Jamilah Lemieux
Location: - Lamond-Riggs/Lillian J. Huff Neighborhood Library
Description: Jamilah Lemieux joins Black Girl Book to discuss her new title, "Black. Single. Mother." This event will celebrate the voices, stories, and brilliance of Black women and girls. Black Girl Booked is a vibrant book club dedicated to celebrating the voices, stories, and brilliance of Black women and girls. Each month, we dive into powerful works—fiction and nonfiction—that center Black female experiences, amplify underrepresented narratives, and spark meaningful conversations. **We’re thrilled to share that author Jamilah Lemieux will join this month’s discussion to talk about her new book *Black. Single. Mother*.** Limited free copies will be available, and Politics and Prose will be on-site for book sales. All are welcomed to attend as we journey together through the many layers, joys, and complexities of motherhood. Whether you're looking to connect, reflect, or simply get lost in a good book, this is your literary home. Join us as we read, uplift, and grow—one page at a time.

Title: Free DC Non-Cooperation Theory + Practice with Empower DC
Location: Location will be shared upon registration
Description: Free DC and Empower DC are partnering to provide this training. Ward 5 residents in particular are invited to attend, and it is open to all who have attended a campaign orientation . Non-Cooperation Theory + Practice is Free DC's advanced training intensive on the key skills involved in effective non-cooperation. This half-day training will teach you what non-cooperation looks like on an individual level, a mass level, and how other movements have used these tactics effectively. Orientation is required Campaign Orientation is a required pre-requisite for this training. If you have not yet attended a Campaign Orientation, you must do so before participating in this training. Join an upcoming Campaign Orientation session » Location This training will take place in-person in DC. Location details will be emailed automatically upon registration. All of Free DC’s training locations are ADA compliant, and are located within a 10-20 minute walk of a Metro station. Interactive Program This training includes interactive role play of protest scenarios. Role play helps us experiment with new skills in safe and supported conditions. If you need accomodations for this or any other part of the training, please let the training team know. Registration details As part of registration for this advanced training we will ask for information about your organizing experience and interest in our work. This step is part of our commitment to safe and supported learning environments. Information collected is confidential and will be shared only with our Training Team. Thank you for helping us get to know you more. Childcare If childcare would support your participation, please indicate that in your registration details and send an email to confirm to erica@freedcproject.org . Interpretation ASL interpretation and Spanish interpretation are both available upon request. If these accommodations would make your participation possible, please indicate that in your registration details and send an email to confirm to erica@freedcproject.org . Se ofrece interpretación ASL e interpretación al español previa solicitud. Si estas adaptaciones hacen posible su participación, indíquelo en sus datos de inscripción y envíe un correo electrónico de confirmación a erica@freedcproject.org . If you have additional questions, contact our Training Team at erica@freedcproject.org . Register below to attend this training!

Title: D.C. WFP Wolf Pack Launch Party!
Location: Sonny’s Pizza, 3120 Georgia Ave NW, Washington, DC, 20010
Description: Whether you’re new to the Working Families Party or a long-time supporter, we invite you to join us for our first in-person D.C. Wolf Pack Launch Party! Title: Living Wage For All — 2nd Annual National Convening
Location: Washington, DC, 20001
Description: # Living Wage For All — 2nd Annual National Convening **Washington, DC | June 27–29, 2026** *The movement is accelerating.* On April 28, 2026, we introduced the **Living Wage For All Act** — the highest federal minimum wage bill in American history. 25 original House cosponsors. 100+ endorsing organizations. 67 million workers covered. And we're fighting to win **$25 and $30 in 2026** across the country through ballot measures and legislation — the highest state and local wage targets ever. Join the Living Wage For All Coalition for three days in Washington, DC: **Saturday, June 27** — NEXT250 Rally for America's 250th Anniversary in DC. Stand with workers from across the country. **June 28–29** — LW4A National Convening. Updates, strategy sessions, and community building — all to win living wages for all workers. Led by the Living Wage For All Coalition Steering Committee and 100+ organizations nationwide. Managed and run by One Fair Wage. **8 States. 9 Campaigns. 17 Million Workers.**

Title: Let's Get Free! March & Concert
Location: Farragut Square, 912 17th St NW, Washington, DC, 20006
Description: In America, power belongs to the people. Right now, an authoritarian movement is threatening our rights, our votes, and our place in this country. They want to divide us, silence us, and rewrite the story of who counts. But there are more of us than there are of them. ### As the Trump administration pushes a national narrative rooted in exclusion, we are coming together to fight for a country where all of us belong. On July 9, we march in Washington, D.C. on the anniversary of the 14th Amendment, the moment when “We the People” expanded to include us all. When everyone has equal protection under the law and every voice counts, it becomes harder to rig elections, hoard power, and rule by fear. As the nation marks 250 years, we are gathering to honor the people who built this country and to defend our freedoms for future generations. We will march at dusk as a nod to everyone who has had to hide in the cover of night, from those fleeing slave catchers to families today hiding from ICE. The march will be followed by a powerful concert headlined by Dead Prez and more! 📍 Farragut Square, Washington, D.C. at 6 p.m. March for your family, your neighbors, and your future. ## Let’s get free!

Title: Lobby Day 2026
Location: 1st St SE, Washington, DC, 20515
Description: Join Color Of Change in D.C. for our 2026 Lobby Day! Calling members in the DMV and surrounding areas to join us at our nation’s capitol on Wednesday, May 20th, 2026, for our 2nd Annual Lobby Day. On the heels of the Supreme Court’s ruling gutting the Voting Rights Act, the time to show up is now. We need to be seen and heard so that our community’s needs are prioritized! Show up, share your story, advocate, and speak with members of Congress and their staff about the issues impacting Black communities with Color Of Change on Wednesday, May 20, 2026, from 8:00 a.m. to - 3:30 pm. Our goal is to bring Black voices directly to those in power. On the heels of the Supreme Court’s ruling gutting the Voting Rights Act, the time to show up is now. We need to be seen and heard so that our community’s needs are prioritized! This year, we will be fighting against: 1. Dangerous book bans in our communities; 2. Threats against our museums and cultural centers; 3. Technology and algorithms that harm–not help–Black communities, families, and voices. That is why we're calling on you to take your message to the hill and advocate for CHANGE with us! RSVP to confirm you will be joining us on the ground for our 2026 Community Lobby Day!

Title: Monthly Committee Meetings, Hybrid & In Person NW DC
Location: Washington, DC, 20008 (address private — RSVP for details)
Description: Join us in NW DC (or via zoom) for a monthly Committee Meeting. We'll be having an inaugural meeting on May 3rd. Drinks and snacks will be served. We will give a brief overview of actions that Indivisible is working on and then we will break into working groups. While working groups are subject to change, we will be focusing on: - Youth Engagement - Election Integrity - Hagerstown Warehouse Camp - Messaging for the 250th Semiquincentennial & Nov. Elections You'll receive documents to review prior to the meeting, but we welcome people to come and just listen to ideas and give their personal feedback on what they like, what they think will work, and if they have ideas for effective action. The youth outreach team is focused on identifying points of contacts in high schools, parent groups, college groups, and third party stakeholders to create a network that allows us how to make our actions more youth-focused/attractive, and how we can provide resources for youth (can we be marshals, can we host sign parties, can we donate space ...) to support their separate organizing efforts. The election integrity team will be helping explain what groups are doing what (like VoPo Pros, Vote Riders) and what it takes to get involved and what kind of work would be done. We can visit about where to focus our time and efforts and continue to find and share the resources popping up around this area. The Hagerstown warehouse is in flux but it is not over. We'll be discussing ways we can help Washington County increase awareness for the ICE facility, how we can help oust the County Commissioners who approved this facility with NO PUBLIC INPUT, and other actions that would stop this ICE facility from coming online. Our 250th Messaging team will be talking about actions we can take that will be engaging and non-confrontational to spread the *true* meaning of the founding of America. We will be thinking of fun, engaging ways to contrast what the vision of freedom looked like in 1776 vs the autocratic regime we have in office in 2026. This meeting will be hybrid. Because it is hybrid, you will have to join via the zoom link. The mobilize link will not automatically take you to the zoom. :) https://us06web.zoom.us/j/84491306603?pwd=fvcgUDxUQMlzK7fsArUeWc4SqNDopv.1 A core principle behind all Indivisible events is a commitment to nonviolent action. Title: Free DC Local Author Conversation with Be Steadwell
Location: Coming soon!
Description: Join Free DC's Popular Education working group in conversation with Be Steadwell, local author of Chocolate Chip City . Singer-songwriter and filmmaker Be Steadwell’s lyrical debut is Practical Magic meets Black Cake in this warm and wry family drama with a magical twist about three sisters, a vision of princes, true love, and revolution, and one very complicated year of self-realization, family dynamics, and learning to let go. Be (they/them, Be) is a queer pop composer and author from Washington DC. Be's goal as an artist is to write love stories centering Black girlies, introverts and weirdos. Be earned a BA in Black Studies from Oberlin College and an MFA in film from Howard University. Be's short films Vow of Silence and Black Tea (2025) screened in film festivals around the world. In 2021 Be released their pop and R&B album- Succulent. In 2023, Be joined the cast of Parable of the Sower the Opera by Toshi Reagon and Dr Bernice Johnson Reagon. Title: Free DC Sound Bath Meditation w/ Jake
Location: Atlas Salon DC, 733 11th Street Northeast
Description: Caring for ourselves and our community is often rooted in the same place. Join us for a morning meditation supporting Free DC. Our monthly Sunday morning Sound Bath Series brings people together for meditation, rest, and community at Third Space @ Atlas. This month, we're partnering with Free DC to explore the connection between personal well-being and community well-being. Through meditation, sound, and stillness, we'll spend an hour reconnecting—with ourselves, with one another, and with the city we call home. Using singing bowls and guided meditation, these sessions are designed to quiet the mind, release tension, and create space for rest, reflection, and connection. Whether you're brand new to meditation or have an established practice, this is a welcoming, accessible space to slow down and simply be. No meditation experience is necessary. Title: Street Law 101: Summer Surge Survival Training
Location: Black Workers & Wellness Center
Description: Ahead of a planned surge of the National Guard, federal and DC police, including ICE, across DC's Black neighborhoods, join the DC COLLAB for a special Street Law 101: Summer Surge Survival Training . This interactive training will help community members, families, organizers, and young people understand what to expect, how increased policing may further impact our neighborhoods, and what rights we have when interacting with cops. You will learn: Your rights during police encounters, stops, frisks, and searches, and how things actually happen in the streets The increased impact on young people and communities of color What to do if you witness police abuse How to document police activity Community-based strategies for protection, support, and rapid response Ways to get involved in building community safety beyond policing These are critical skills for the People's Self-Defense Network that we are building together. We'll be enjoying snacks throughout and a discussion will follow the screening. If you have any questions about this event don't hesitate to reach out to Leila or Jillian at organize@harrietsdreams.org

Title: Campaign Orientation (Virtual)
Location: Virtual Event
Description: Free DC is setting out to build the cultural and political power it will take to protect Home Rule and win lasting dignity for the people of DC. Campaign Orientation is the place to learn about the attacks DC is facing right now, our strategy to defeat them, and our organizing structures that will make it possible. Register below to attend!

Title: Harriets Responders Orientation
Location: Virtual Event
Description: Come learn more about the Responders of Harriet's Wildest Dreams! Harriets Responders is committed to building local power through transformational direct actions, rapid response & mutual aid. By organizing, strategizing and responding, we work to decrease the power of the carceral state and oppressive systems. Join this info session to learn how to get plugged in to the DC Safety Squad and Harriets Responders. Come learn about how we are building community and reimagining safety. We are piloting a response team that is going to act as an alternatives to calling the police. We are advocating for harm reduction resources, as we provide community support, perform rapid responses and civil disobedience. #WeKeepUsSafe

Title: HWD General Info Session
Location: Virtual Event
Description: Join us for a snapshot of the work Harriet’s Wildest Dreams is engaged in. Our liberation is tied to one another, and we need each other to do this work to protect our people — Black people, formerly incarcerated folks, queer and trans communities, and all those at risk of state-sanctioned violence. Being in the nation's capital, we are at the center of the empire and also a target for far-right extremists. DC is NO ONE’S playground, and we must remain vigilant in our fight for statehood, our fight against mass incarceration, and the fight for gender liberation and bodily autonomy for all. If you and your friends are interested in building a better world, free from patriarchal violence, a world where queerness is in, then you've come to the right place! Come sit with us as we re-imagine what this world can be and reclaim safety for all of DC. 👉🏽 This month's info session will feature a mini political education session on the impact of the Trump administration's DC Safe and Beautiful Ta

Title: Chocolate City Orientation
Location: Ward 5 Community Space
Description: Join the Free DC Chocolate City Group for political education, strategy, and community conversation. Get updates on the Free DC campaign, connect with neighbors, and organize for the fights ahead. Whether you’re new or already involved, pull up.

Title: Marshal & De-Escalation Training
Location: Virtual Event
Description: A training to learn tools to be a affective Marshall for Free DC Events. Marshals work as a team to keep people as safe as possible by bringing them together and order in an appropriate or effective way. Their primary work is to support a safe and successful event as a primary point of contact with event participants. Marshals should communicate safety preparedness and are to report all safety and emergency issues to Action Leads.

Title: Juror Information Project
Location: Sign up for more details.
Description: Harriet’s Wildest Dreams and Free DC are coming together for a juror teach-in. We’ll discuss what it really means to serve on a jury and how we can use that role to protect our people especially those with marginalized identities who are disproportionately targeted by the criminal legal system. Jury duty is not just a civic responsibility; it’s a powerful tool for ensuring fairness and justice. As community members, our participation in juries is vital to safeguarding the rights of those who are most vulnerable to systemic biases. By serving on a jury, we can influence outcomes and help create a more equitable legal process. Come and engage in meaningful conversations, learn from legal experts, activists, and fellow community members, and find out how you can play a pivotal role in shaping a more just society. This session is open to everyone.

Title: Free DC Beyond Allyship: How to be an Accomplice for a Free DC
Location: Zoom link sent upon registration
Description: What does it mean to move beyond allyship and into accompliceship and meaningful action? As we navigate the aftermath of the DC Democratic primary, a summer of heightened National Guard and federal police presence, and escalating threats from the administration, this virtual teach-in will explore what this political moment demands of all of us. Together, we'll ground ourselves in the history of policing and abolition, examine DC's long tradition of community resistance and non-cooperation, and discuss how these lessons can inform our organizing today. Title: FILM SCREENING: Las Muertes Más Bellas del Mundo
Location: Sankofa Video Books & Café
Description: Join Sankofa Video Books & Café for an evening film screening under the stars on their patio presented in collaboration with Positive Productions Inc. Follow stories of loss, healing, and new possibilities from the Salvadoran D.C. diaspora in “Las Muertes Más Bellas del Mundo/The Most Beautiful Deaths in the World”, an artistically crafted feature-length documentary from Quique Aviles and the Yellow House Collective. The film follows Quique on his journey to find answers and healing from the traumatic events that led to his migration north, leading him to write "Las Muertes Más Bellas del Mundo," a powerful, heart-wrenching poem that serves to reclaim his dead and the dignity of a nation torn apart by war and mass displacement. Threaded within Quique's journey are interviews, music videos, performances, and archival footage of Salvadoran-Wachintonian artists that tell an intimate story of a community resolving trauma and finding identity, belonging, and joy after fleeing the Salvadoran

If you are interested in being trained as a legal observer, please register for one of the training dates below. Title: Socialist Night School: Socialism 101
Location: Martin Luther King Jr. Memorial Library, Room 401-A
Description: Socialism is in the air, and on the march. Democrats Socialists like Zohran Mandami in New York City and Janeese Lewis George in DC are winning elections. More and more people are embracing socialism. But what is is it? In our next Socialist Night School, we’ll tackle the question of socialism. What is capitalism? What is democracy? What is socialism? How does the work of DSA help us get there? How can you get involved (or more involved) in DSA? While everyone is welcome, this session is especially designed for newer members and those who are relatively new to socialism. The session was led by Socialist Night School co-organizer David K. Questions? Accessibility concerns? Email politicaleducation@mdcdsa.org . NOTE: This event will be recorded and may be shared on MDC DSA’s social media platforms for educational purposes. Title: Slow Stitching Our Collective Stories
Location: The Festival Center, 1640 Columbia Rd NW, Washington, DC 20009
Description: Windows of Liberation: Art, Memory & Sacred Storytelling Join artist AnaYelsi Velasco Sanchez, current exhibiting artist at the Festival Center, for an interactive workshop exploring justice, memory, healing, and collective possibility through art. We’ll begin with a guided justice timeline experience exploring histories of injustice, resistance, and survival in the United States. Please note: this portion includes sensitive material related to violence, oppression, and systemic injustice. Participants will then create a layered stained-glass inspired “window of liberation” using symbolism and reflection to honor stories of struggle, survival, healing, and hope. No art experience needed. Come make art. Come reflect. Come imagine liberation. RSVP Ticket About the JAM Program The Justice, Art & Movement (JAM) program is the Festival Center’s year-round arts and justice initiative, bringing together artists whose work explores liberation, identity, community, and social transformation. Title: Free DC Local Author Conversation with Be Steadwell
Location: Festival Center, 1640 Columbia Rd NW 2nd Floor, Washington, DC 20009
Description: Join Free DC's Popular Education working group in conversation with Be Steadwell, local author of Chocolate Chip City . Singer-songwriter and filmmaker Be Steadwell’s lyrical debut is Practical Magic meets Black Cake in this warm and wry family drama with a magical twist about three sisters, a vision of princes, true love, and revolution, and one very complicated year of self-realization, family dynamics, and learning to let go. Be (they/them, Be) is a queer pop composer and author from Washington DC. Be's goal as an artist is to write love stories centering Black girlies, introverts and weirdos. Be earned a BA in Black Studies from Oberlin College and an MFA in film from Howard University. Be's short films Vow of Silence and Black Tea (2025) screened in film festivals around the world. In 2021 Be released their pop and R&B album- Succulent. In 2023, Be joined the cast of Parable of the Sower the Opera by Toshi Reagon and Dr Bernice Johnson Reagon. Title: Free DC The DC Occupation: A Discussion with Local Veterans
Location: Lutheran Church of the Reformation (Virtual also available), 212 East Capitol Street Northeast
Description: We've had National Guard troops on DC's streets for nearly a year, and the recently announced DC Safe and Beautiful Task Force summer surge will bring an additional 5,000 troops into the city. Many of us have questions about what deployment actually looks like, what choices National Guard members have, the consequences they could face for refusing orders, how to engage with them effectively, and what strategies residents can use to push back against the ongoing occupation. To help answer those questions, members of Free DC's Veterans Constituency Group will be joined by Common Defense, a national organization of progressive veterans and military families that advocates for democracy, civil rights, and responsible national security policy. Together, they'll provide a National Guard 101 presentation covering how the Guard operates, the realities of these deployments, and the legal and practical considerations surrounding them.
